exemplar.title:
Title: Evaluating Swedenâs immigration policy

Research Question: How effective has Swedenâs immigration integration program been since the 2010s?

detailedExemplarData.title:
Title: Evaluating Swedenâs immigration policy

Research Question: How effective has Swedenâs immigration integration program been since the 2010s?

strengths: Topic is communicated clearly and appropriately via title and introduction.
Research question is explicitly stated and provides a coherent line of inquiry.
Methodology selection (secondary quantitative/qualitative studies, case studies, statistics) is appropriate for an evaluative policy study.
weaknesses: Key term âeffectiveâ is not operationalized, limiting precision of focus.
Rationale for method selection is asserted rather than critically justified.
Scope remains broad (multiple policy areas and time frames), blurring investigative boundaries.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work fulfills the descriptors for the 3â4 band: the topic and question are clearly presented, and methods are generally suitable, but the focus is only partially tight and methodological justification is limited. Awarding 4 reflects performance at the upper end of this band.

strengths: Source material is extensive, current (2021â2024), and clearly relevant to each sub-section.
Knowledge of integration policy and psychological constructs is coherent and applied effectively, with interdisciplinary examples.
Terminology (e.g., SFI, PTSD, acculturation) is used accurately and consistently throughout.
weaknesses: Discussion occasionally remains descriptive rather than deeply theoretical (e.g., limited engagement with integration models).
Minor informal phrasing appears and some empirical data (e.g., participation rates) are missing to support assertions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source selection and relevance align with the 5â6 band, and knowledge is integrated effectively though not yet exemplary. A score of 5 acknowledges strong performance with room for deeper theoretical integration.

strengths: Research draws on a wide range of reputable statistics, peer-reviewed studies, and policy documents, situating Swedenâs policy in a global context.
Argument is structured thematically and maintains cohesion across therapy, culture, language, and employment sections.
Comparative and quantitative data (OECD comparisons, cross-national stats) strengthen analysis relevance.
weaknesses: Analysis often summarizes source findings without fully synthesizing them into causal arguments.
Critical evaluation of programmes (e.g., methodological limitations, participant feedback) is noted but not rigorously developed.
Conclusion reiterates section summaries without directly answering the research question in a synthesized final judgment.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates coherent, largely supported analysis and structured argument fitting the upper range of the 7â9 band; awarding 10 recognizes its strong critical engagement despite occasional lack of depth and nuance.

strengths: Clear academic structure with coherent headings, functioning table of contents, and logical chapter divisions.
Visual elements (diagrams of SFI and SFX structures) are well chosen, correctly labeled, and captioned.
Table of contents and sub-headings facilitate navigation and reinforce logical flow.
weaknesses: Referencing format is inconsistent (mix of MLA parenthetical elements and numbered footnotes).
Some figures are not referenced in the main text, weakening integration of visuals.
Minor inconsistencies in section numbering and occasional abrupt transitions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the 3â4 band descriptors for academic structure and visual integration but falls short on consistent referencing and seamless formatting. A score of 3 accurately reflects this level of performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows a logical thematic structure (therapy, culture, language, employment) and usually maintains cohesion. Some critical comparison of programmes appears, and opposing viewpoints are acknowledged. Nevertheless, the interdisciplinary synthesis (Psychology + Global Politics) is modest, and evaluative depth is limited; counter-arguments are noted but not rigorously weighed. The argument is coherent but lacks the nuance and sustained critical interrogation required for the higher bands, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

A full bibliography is provided and most in-text citations use superscript numbers; however, the list is labeled âMLAâ while the formatting resembles a mix of MLA and web-link annotations, and some entries lack consistent punctuation or hanging indents. In-text citations do not follow MLA parenthetical conventions, and there are occasional missing page numbers. These inconsistencies place the work in the 1-2 band, with evidence of referencing but limited adherence to a recognized standard, warranting a score of 2.

The student identifies a relevant and clearly stated topicâSwedenâs immigration-integration policyâand conveys it through the title, abstract elements and introduction. However, the scope remains quite broad, covering four distinct policy areas over more than a decade, so the precise boundaries of investigation (time frame, population, criteria for âeffectivenessâ) are only partially delineated. Occasional shifts from 2010s to post-2021 further blur the focus. Overall communication is understandable but not consistently sharp, aligning best with the middle-band descriptor.

The essay is organised with a clear title page, abstract-like introduction, logical chapter headings and a functioning table of contents. Paragraphing is generally coherent, and visual material is embedded with captions. Minor inconsistencies in section numbering and occasional abrupt transitions prevent full clarity, but the structure overall meets academic expectations, aligning with the 3-4 band at a solid score of 3.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of Swedenâs integration structures, policies and associated psychological concepts, weaving statistics and case studies into the narrative. Connections between disciplines (e.g., mental-health research interpreted through policy lenses) are present but sometimes descriptive rather than fully integrated. Occasional factual generalisations and limited depth of theoretical engagement restrain the work to a competent, not exemplary, levelâfitting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Analytical sections move beyond description by comparing Swedish outcomes with other countries, evaluating policy reforms and noting limitations (e.g., time constraints in SFI). However, analysis often summarises source findings rather than synthesising them; causal links are asserted more than demonstrated, and the final conclusion largely reiterates section summaries without a nuanced overall judgment. Support for evaluative statements is present but uneven, corresponding to the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The research questionââHow effective has Sweden's immigration integration program been since the 2010s?ââis explicitly stated and provides a clear line of inquiry. Nevertheless, it combines multiple dimensions (therapeutic care, cultural orientation, language teaching, vocational education) under one umbrella, so the focus is only partially tight; key terms such as âeffectiveâ are not operationalized. The question is serviceable but not fully refined or delimited, fitting the 3-4 band for being clear yet only partly focused.

The investigation draws on a range of national statistics, peer-reviewed studies and policy documents that are generally appropriate and up-to-date. While not every claim is equally substantiated, the bulk of evidence aligns well with the sub-questions, showing deliberate effort to obtain relevant research. Some over-reliance on secondary summaries and popular media keeps it below the top band, but the overall quality merits a strong mid-level (5/6) score.

The essay outlines an interdisciplinary approach (Global Politics and Psychology) and relies on secondary quantitative/qualitative studies, case studies and official statisticsâmethods that are generally appropriate to an evaluative policy study. However, the rationale for choosing these particular methods and how they will be synthesised is only briefly asserted and not critically justified; primary data collection is absent. The methodology is therefore adequate but not âeffective and well informedâ, meriting the mid-band score.

The bibliography is extensive (70+ entries) and includes academic journals, government documents, international-agency reports and reputable news outlets directly related to Swedish immigration and integration. Sources are generally current (many from 2021-2024) and clearly pertinent to each sub-section, showing deliberate, informed selection. Minor reliance on journalistic pieces prevents a perfect 6, but overall relevance and breadth align with the top band.

Two relevant diagrams are included, each captioned and referenced. The images enhance understanding of SFI and SFX structures. Labels are clear and sources cited, satisfying rubric expectations. Design is straightforward rather than sophisticated, but effectiveness is evident; thus the work meets the 3-4 band, meriting a 3.

Key terms such as "integration", "SFI", "civic orientation" and psychological constructs (e.g., PTSD, stigma) are used accurately and usually consistently. The student shows understanding of interdisciplinary concepts, but deeper theoretical discussion (e.g., models of integration, acculturation theory) is limited, and occasional informal phrasing appears. This places performance solidly within the competent 3-4 band.

annotations.comment:
A cross-sectional survey is cited without detailing sample representativeness or response bias. The student should reference methodological limitations (e.g.
self-report bias) to strengthen critical analysis.

The abbreviations list demonstrates accurate use of disciplinary terms, but the footnote formatting varies (e.g., spacing and font size). The student should standardize superscript and reference layout across entries.

The abbreviations section reflects consistent and precise use of key terminology (e.g.
EU, PTSD, SFI, UNHCR), showing solid conceptual understanding and aiding reader comprehension of technical terms.
The table of contents presents coherent chapter headings and page references, facilitating navigation through the essay. Its clear academic structure aligns well with IB presentation expectations.

While the civic orientation curriculum is described, the student omits evaluation of its real-world effectiveness (e.g.
participant feedback, outcome measures). Including such data would deepen the analysis.

The vocational training discussion presents employment rates but does not explore the overqualification issue in depth. The student should analyze why refugees accept roles below their qualifications.

Comparative mental-health statistics (e.g.
Syrian depression vs.
Norwegian PTSD rates) demonstrate effective application of source data and enrich the analysis with cross-national perspectives.

The discussion on late psychiatric care and cultural stigma includes recent studies and highlights nuanced barriers to access, demonstrating good integration of interdisciplinary psychological research.
The case study of Fawaz is interesting but generalizes from a single family. The student should acknowledge limitations and avoid implying these findings represent all refugee experiences.
The figure caption is informative, but the student does not reference Figure 2 in the main text. Adding in-text mentions will integrate the visual into the argument more effectively.
The bibliography mixes MLA parenthetical elements with numbered footnotes, lacking consistent style. The student should adopt a single referencing format and apply it uniformly.

The title effectively communicates the investigationâs focus on Swedenâs integration policy. To sharpen the scope further, the student should specify the exact timeframe and define key metrics for âeffectivenessâ directly in the title.
Including a word count at the start meets the formatting requirement and shows awareness of IB guidelines. This clear presentation aids evaluators in confirming compliance with the 4,000-word limit.

The methodology overview mentions interdisciplinary approaches but lacks justification for choosing quantitative over qualitative sources in certain sections. The student should explain why these methods best address the RQ.

The research question is clearly stated but uses the term âeffectiveâ without operationalization. The student should define specific indicators (e.g.
employment rates, language proficiency levels) to make the question fully focused.

The introduction provides extensive context but remains too broad, covering global displacement and multiple policy changes. To strengthen focus, the student should delimit the timeframe or population (e.g.
post-2021) more precisely.

The student integrates global statistics and OECD comparisons to situate Swedenâs policy within a broader context. This use of reputable quantitative data enhances the researchâs quality and relevance.
The sub-question header for therapeutic help is clearly labeled and well integrated into the structure, reinforcing the essayâs logical flow.
The section on cultural integration correctly identifies the complexity of defining the term and draws on diverse academic sources, demonstrating a solid grasp of conceptual debates.

The analysis acknowledges subjective definitions of cultural integration but lacks theoretical grounding (e.g.
Berryâs acculturation model). Incorporating such frameworks would deepen critical insight.
The critique of the mandatory introduction courseâs complexity for children shows strong evaluative capability and links program design to target audience comprehensionâgood critical thinking.
The discussion of course duration would benefit from empirical participation data. Adding attendance or completion rates would strengthen evidence of how time constraints affect outcomes.
The âCivic Orientationâ sub-heading is clearly formatted and indicates a logical sub-division of the broader cultural integration theme, aiding reader navigation.
Figure 1 clearly visualizes the SFI level divisions and study paths. The caption is informative, and the diagram enhances comprehension of the programâs structure.
The historical overview of SFI reforms lacks a critical assessment of how each policy change affected learning outcomes. The student should link reforms to measurable impacts.
The vocational education sub-question is clearly marked and signals a shift to economic integration, maintaining consistent academic structure with earlier chapters.
Figure 2 provides a detailed timeline of SFX program stages and durations, supporting the textâs description and helping readers visualize the accelerated pathway.

The conclusion summarizes sections but does not directly answer the research question or synthesize insights into a clear final evaluation. The student should articulate a focused conclusion that addresses the RQ explicitly.
